PersonalBudgetingSoftware
  • main
  • main.models module
  • View page source

main.models module

Database models for the main budgeting application.

This module defines the application’s primary Django models: Profile, Transaction, Budget, and Goal. Models include convenience properties used by templates and views.

class main.models.Profile(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

User profile information attached to a Django User.

user

One-to-one link to the Django User instance.

Type:

User

phone

Optional phone number string.

Type:

str

date_of_birth

Optional date of birth.

Type:

date

budget_alert_threshold

Percentage threshold used to trigger budget alerts (0-100).

Type:

int

class main.models.Transaction(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

A financial transaction record.

user

Owner of the transaction.

Type:

User

amount

Monetary amount for the transaction. This field uses a deferred-loading database implementation; the query is executed the first time the value is accessed. Use help_text and verbose_name on the field to surface human-friendly text in admin pages and documentation.

Type:

Decimal

category

Category name for grouping transactions (e.g., “Food”, “Rent”).

Type:

str

type

Either ‘income’ or ‘expense’.

Type:

str

name

Optional short title for the transaction.

Type:

str

description

Optional long description.

Type:

str

note

Optional internal note.

Type:

str

date

Timestamp when the transaction was created.

Type:

datetime

TRANSACTION_TYPES = [('income', 'Income'), ('expense', 'Expense')]
property transaction_type

Alias property for template compatibility.

Some templates expect transaction.transaction_type while the database field is named type. This property returns the same value as the type field.

class main.models.Budget(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

A budget tied to a user and category.

user

Owner of the budget.

Type:

User

category

Budget category name.

Type:

str

amount_limit

The maximum allowed spending for the budget period.

Type:

Decimal

period

The date representing the budgeting period (e.g., month start).

Type:

date

class main.models.Goal(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

A saving or financial goal for a user.

user

Owner of the goal.

Type:

User

title

A short title for the goal.

Type:

str

target_amount

The target amount to reach.

Type:

Decimal

current_amount

Current saved amount toward the target.

Type:

Decimal

deadline

Optional target date to reach the goal.

Type:

date

category

Category for the goal (defaults to ‘Savings’).

Type:

str

created_at

Timestamp when the goal was created.

Type:

datetime

property progress_percentage

Calculate and return the goal progress as an integer percentage.

The value is clamped to 100 and returns 0 if target_amount is zero or not set.

main.views module

main.views.main(request)[source]

Render the public landing page.

Parameters:

request – Django HttpRequest object.

Returns:

HttpResponse rendering the index template.

main.views.signup_view(request)[source]

Handle new user signups.

Validates form fields, creates a new User instance and logs the user in on successful registration. Uses Django messages for errors.

main.views.login_view(request)[source]

Authenticate a user and start a session.

Accepts POST with email and password. On success redirects to the dashboard; otherwise adds an error message and re-renders the login form.

main.views.dashboard_view(request)[source]

Render the authenticated user’s dashboard.

The dashboard aggregates recent transactions, budgets and goals to present an overview of the user’s financial state.

main.views.transactions_view(request)[source]

List, create and update user transactions.

GET: returns a filtered list of transactions. Supports type and search query params.

POST: creates a new transaction or updates an existing one when edit_id is provided. Validation errors are reported via messages and redirect back to the transactions page.

main.views.delete_transaction(request, pk)[source]

Delete a transaction owned by the current user.

Parameters:

  • request – Django HttpRequest object.

  • pk – Primary key of the transaction to delete.

Returns:

HttpResponse redirecting to the transaction list.

main.views.budgets_view(request)[source]

Manage monthly budgets for the current user.

GET: renders the budgets for the current month. POST: creates or updates a budget for a category for the current month and shows a confirmation message.

main.views.goals_view(request)[source]

Create, edit, and add funds to financial goals.

Supports multiple POST actions identified by keys in the POST data (e.g., add_funds, edit_goal, delete_goal, add_goal).

main.views.reports_view(request)[source]

Generate financial reports and export them as PDF.

The view computes aggregates for income, expenses, budgets and goals over a date range (default last 30 days) and builds an insights list with personalized advice. If export_pdf is in POST, attempts to create a PDF using xhtml2pdf with a WeasyPrint fallback.

main.views.profile(request)[source]

Display and update the current user’s profile.

Handles updating personal info, changing password, deleting the account, and setting the budget alert threshold. Uses Django messages to inform the user of success or validation errors.

main.views.logout_view(request)[source]

Log out the current user and redirect to the login page.

Previous
